classification.plan_type_rationale: Mechanical test+CI work with a known seed (existing dual-AC test at __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts:98-241). Three change targets identified at scoping time: (1) frozen on-disk fixture under __tests__/fixtures/ (or inline), (2) anti-tautology guard, (3) CI wiring (vitest/package.json). No investigation phase needed; seed fixture already exercises both grains correctly.
classification.plan_type_decided_by: SKILLS scoping (DISPATCH-045 dispatch payload: 'Plan_type likely simple_implementation OR phased_implementation depending on whether SKILLS judges fixture-shape decision needs an investigation phase'). SKILLS judges no investigation needed — the seed test is the working reference.
classification.discovery: Seed test at __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts:98-241 already constructs a 3×3 fixture in code (NUM_FILES=3, NODES_PER_FILE=3, MISSING_PER_FILE=2 → 6 element-grain skips, 3 file-grain unique). The fixture is procedural (built each test run from constants) rather than frozen on-disk. This WO promotes it to a frozen artifact.

in_scope: Author a frozen on-disk fixture under src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/ (graph.json + validation-report.json + 3 source files mod0.ts/mod1.ts/mod2.ts) capturing the same 3×3 shape the seed test builds in code

Refactor the AC-05 dual-identity test (currently at __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts:98-241) to load the frozen fixture from disk OR replace with a new test under src/integration/rag/__tests__/ that uses it
Add an anti-tautology guard: expected counts (6 element-grain, 3 file-grain) must come from independent computation in the test, not from the fixture's runtime output
Update vitest config / package.json scripts so the dual-AC test runs in default `vitest run` and is blocking (it already does — verify, do not assume)
Document the fixture invariant relationship in a fixture README.md: skipped element count === graph nodes with hs=missing; uniqueFiles(skipDetails).size === validation_report.header_missing_count

Boundary clarification: the seed test lives at __tests__/pipeline/, not src/integration/rag/__tests__/. WO scope explicitly extends to BOTH __tests__/pipeline/* AND src/integration/rag/__tests__/* AND a new fixture dir, plus minimal CI wiring (vitest config or package.json)

evidence_summary.seed_fixture_shape: 3 files × 3 nodes/file = 9 total nodes; 2 missing + 1 defined per file = 6 element-grain hs=missing, 3 file-grain unique
evidence_summary.what_changes_in_this_wo: The 3×3 input is currently built procedurally in the test from JS constants. This WO promotes it to a frozen on-disk artifact (graph.json + validation-report.json + source files) so the inputs are inspectable, diffable in PRs, and immune to silent test-helper drift. The assertions stay strict; the anti-tautology guard ensures expected counts come from independent computation.
evidence_summary.ci_wiring_baseline_check: Default `vitest run` already picks up __tests__/pipeline/* per current config — WO must verify and document, not assume
evidence_summary.real_world_anchor_numbers_at_predecessor: AC-05a 2415===2415, AC-05b 263===263 (NOT load-bearing for this WO; the fixture uses 6/3 and asserts the identity relationship at smaller scale)
